steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the type of words whose frequency a corpus can determine. The parenthetical contrast is the key: "Words used mainly in specialized or academic publications... tend to be poorly represented." So the blank must describe the opposite — words that ARE well-represented, i.e., common, everyday words. The example ("world" as the eighth most common noun) confirms this.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'trivial': Means unimportant or insignificant. Describes low value, not high frequency. "World" isn't trivial — it's one of the most common nouns. ✗
  • Choice B — 'conventional': Means standard, ordinary, commonly used. Conventional words are the kind well-represented in a general corpus — the opposite of specialized academic vocabulary. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'profound': Means deep or significant. Profound words would be rare and academic — closer to the contrast side, not the blank side. ✗
  • Choice D — 'accidental': Means unintentional or random. Irrelevant to how frequently words appear in a corpus. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
